title: The biggest philosophical question of our time: superstition vrs Science

transcript.text: I was thinking lately, what is the biggest philosophical question of our time?
And I think it is finding out exactly where the very foggy line is between superstition and real science, I.e. dogma and real science.
I think, you know, all of these various superstitions did actually arise from something very real.
For example, the superstition, don't walk under a ladder.
You know, I'm sure back in the day, lots of people were walking under ladders and getting stuff dropped on their head or whatever and getting hurt.
So then they just said, well, just don't walk under a ladder. And then people just didn't do it.

And then it turned into dogma, superstition, and then people would start saying, start thinking that, you know, you don't walk under a ladder because of some phantom reason like, you know, when it's not, you know, black and white.
And so I think originally, the original creation of superstitions is good, but if you get stuck on them, then they get carried away into a world where they don't belong.
You know, we have to realize that we're moving on something that's moving.
We're on a wave, on a wave, on a wave that's on a wave.
And, you know, it's kind of like being nostalgic about a song, you know.
80s beats sound good for the 80s, but maybe not for now, you know.
Now we've got . So, you know, back in the 80s, a for a beat would sound crazy.
So, the big problem, you know, obviously, is so many people in third world countries, like Africa and stuff, get caught up in superstition.
You know, if an owl flies in the room, oh my god, somebody's gonna die, somebody's gonna die.
And then you halt your whole life, and there's a wrench in everything.
But, on the other side, I think scientists get dogmatic about their beliefs too.
And they would automatically not believe in astrology and say that you can't do experiments on it, when in fact you can.
You just have to wait for placements to be there again.
And it's a very fuzzy line, which is a beautiful thing, because we all get to bang our heads against the wall and think and think and think, which is the game of life.
But, where, what, I think that will be the big question, is just putting a pinpoint on learning how to think for ourselves.
Because a lot of us just believe stuff because somebody said it.
So, that's basically all I have to say.
I think that's the big problem, and you have to take things.
But, I have a bunch of examples.
I will upload them.
I will upload them.
I will upload them.
I will upload them.
